title: Neonatal brachial artery pseudoaneurysm after PICC placement
description: A 39-week term, 41-day-old male infant with hypoxic-ischemic encephalopathy and Lennox-Gastaut syndrome developed a rapidly enlarging pulsatile mass on the medial right upper extremity. Imaging confirmed a pseudoaneurysm of the right distal brachial artery, likely related to prior PICC placement, which was treated with microscope-assisted resection and end-to-end arterial repair followed by short-course heparin. This case exercises all six workflow stages on a neonatal vascular surgery and neurology scenario derived from the AGBonnet augmented-clinical-notes dataset (idx 32955; conversation and full_note columns).
notes: From (idx:32955) (augmented-clinical-notes). All patient data is fully synthetic — no real patient information.
